title: Ensure That the ‘Log_min_duration_statement’ Database  Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to '-1' (Disabled)

input_row.title: Ensure That the ‘Log_min_duration_statement’ Database  Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to '-1' (Disabled)
input_row.description: The log_min_duration_statement flag defines the minimum amount of execution time of a statement in milliseconds where the total duration of the statement is logged. Ensure that log_min_duration_statement is disabled, i.e., a value of -1 is set.
input_row.rationale: Logging SQL statements may include sensitive information that should not be recorded in logs. This recommendation is applicable to PostgreSQL database instances.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview page. 3. Go to the Configuration card. 4. Under Database flags, check that the value of log_min_duration_statement flag is set to -1. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Use the below command for every Cloud SQL PostgreSQL database instance to verify the value of log_min_duration_statement is set to -1. gcloud sql instances describe --format=json| jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_duration_statement")|.value' In the output, database flags are listed under the settings as the collection databaseFlags.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the PostgreSQL instance where the database flag needs to be enabled. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add item, choose the flag log_min_duration_statement from the drop-down menu and set a value of -1. 6. Click Save. 7. Confirm the changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud SQL database instances using the following command: gcloud sql instances list 2. Configure the log_min_duration_statement flag for every Cloud SQL PosgreSQL database instance using the below command: gcloud sql instances patch --database-flags log_min_duration_statement=-1 Note: This command will overwrite all database flags previously set. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags to be set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("=").
input_row.impact: Turning on logging will increase the required storage over time. Mismanaged logs may cause your storage costs to increase. Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.additional_information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note: Some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note: Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be fully verified using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and GCP CLI.
- The audit description includes a CLI command that checks the value of the `log_min_duration_statement` flag, further supporting automation.
- The rationale does not require subjective human judgment, and there are clear steps provided for verification.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_log_min_duration_statement_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command `gcloud sql instances describe --format=json` to retrieve the configuration for each Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance.
- The command will pull instance settings, including the `databaseFlags`.
- The script should enumerate all Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances available within the project and check the `log_min_duration_statement` flag value.
- Expected configuration properties to validate:
  - Validate that the `value` of `log_min_duration_statement` is set to `-1`.
  
**Pass Criteria:**
- If `log_min_duration_statement` is set to `-1`, the compliance check passes.
- If `log_min_duration_statement` is set to any value other than `-1`, the compliance check fails.

**Example Implementation:**
```bash
# List all Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances and check the log_min_duration_statement value
gcloud sql instances list --format=json | jq '.[] | select(.databaseVersion == "POSTGRES_13") | {name: .name, log_min_duration_statement: (.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_duration_statement") | .value)}'
```
- This command validates all PostgreSQL instances by checking the specific flag setting, pulling in necessary information for compliance.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be fully verified using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and GCP CLI, as the task involves retrieving and checking configuration values that are accessible via these tools.
- The use of the `gcloud sql instances describe` command is appropriate for accessing the necessary configuration details, specifically the `databaseFlags` for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly correct. The `gcloud sql instances describe` command is valid and can retrieve the necessary configuration details for each Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance.
- The example implementation uses `gcloud sql instances list` with `jq` to filter and format the output, which is a valid approach. However, the command assumes all instances are PostgreSQL and does not account for instances without the `log_min_duration_statement` flag set.
- The program name `gcp_sql_instance_log_min_duration_statement_disabled` is descriptive and follows the convention, but could be slightly more concise.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- The command should include a check to ensure that the `log_min_duration_statement` flag exists before attempting to access its value. This can prevent errors if the flag is not set for some instances.
- Consider using `gcloud sql instances describe` directly on each instance rather than filtering the list output, which can be more efficient and less error-prone.
- The program name could be improved for clarity and brevity: `gcp_sql_postgresql_log_min_duration_statement_check`.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded and uses appropriate GCP tools and methods to automate the compliance check. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the approach is sound.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Modify the example implementation to include a check for the existence of the `log_min_duration_statement` flag before attempting to access its value.
- Consider revising the program name to `gcp_sql_postgresql_log_min_duration_statement_check` for improved clarity and conciseness.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that the control can be automated using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and GCP CLI. The task involves checking configuration values that are accessible via these tools, specifically the `log_min_duration_statement` flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances. The necessary properties are exposed through GCP APIs, allowing for an automated approach without requiring manual inspection.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_postgresql_log_min_duration_statement_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instances
- **API/CLI Method:** Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) `gcloud sql instances describe --format=json`
- **Properties to Check:** `settings.databaseFlags` for the `log_min_duration_statement` flag
- **Pass Criteria:** The `log_min_duration_statement` flag is set to `-1`
- **Fail Criteria:** The `log_min_duration_statement` flag is set to any value other than `-1` or is not set

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions. Both supported the automated approach, and the second opinion provided additional validation and minor improvements to the automation details, which have been incorporated into the final decision.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
